title: Eulerian Path vs. Eulerian Circuit
description: A visual comparison of Eulerian and non-Eulerian graphs, highlighting the key concepts of vertex degree and traversability.
relationships: Graph A has all vertices with even degree, allowing a cycle that starts and ends at the same vertex.
Graph B has exactly two vertices with odd degree, allowing a path that starts at one odd vertex and ends at the other.
Graph C has more than two vertices with odd degree, making it non-traversable in a single path.
